expectations.text: internalEdges contains exactly ui→shared and api→shared as {from, to} objects using layer names — no duplicates, no self-edges
no invented edges: nothing into or out of db (api does not depend on db in this fixture) and no shared→ui/api reversals

externalDeps per layer name the defining deps only: ui includes react, api includes express, db includes prisma (@prisma/client), shared includes zod — and stays a handful, not a dump of every devDependency
ordering identifies shared as a provider consumed by ui and api, stating it changes first in cross-layer tasks
expectations.evidence: ordering={"providers":["shared","db"],"consumers":["api","ui"],"dispatchOrder":["shared","db","api","ui"],"rationale":"shared is depended on by both apps (ui and api declare \"shared\": \"*\"), so contract/schema changes there must land first. db owns the Prisma schema and is a provider by role even though no package currently consumes it. api and ui are leaf consumers; ui last since it sits at the presentation edge."}

expectations.text: report validates structurally: internalEdges entries pass the profile schema rules (from/to are known layer names) and gaps is an empty array
expectations.evidence: non-layer-name edges=[], gaps=["db is not declared as a dependency by any other workspace package — the expected api -> db edge is absent, so db is currently an island; its provider position in dispatch ordering is inferred from role, not from a manifest edge.","ui and api declare a dependency on shared in package.json but no source file actually imports from it — declared edge is unexercised in code.","No tsconfig.json anywhere despite .ts/.tsx sources, and all build scripts are echo stubs — the TypeScript toolchain wiring cannot be verified.","No internal edge into ui from anywhere (expected: ui is a pure consumer), and api does not call db or expose data routes beyond /health, so the runtime data flow ui -> api -> db is implied by layer roles only, not by imports."]
